item.title: Australia | Latest News Headlines | Nine.com.au
item.description: The latest Australia news, articles and analysis from Nine.com.au

item.title: Middle East | Latest News Headlines | Nine.com.au
item.description: The latest Middle East news, articles and analysis from Nine.com.au

item.title: Briana went to an online meeting. Her team mate sent an AI assistant
item.description: Recently, something odd happened to Briana Blackett. She was attending a meeting to give a colleague some content feedback, which isn't unusual in her volunteer work as a disability advocate.

item.title: Drag x Drive review: The new chaotic game that is easy to pick up but incredibly difficult to master
item.description: Drag x Drive wants to be Rocket League; one of my favourite games of all time. In concept, it nails the basics.

item.title: Call of Duty: Black Ops 7 revealed
item.description: Activision has officially lifted the lid on Call of Duty: Black Ops 7, the next entry in the blockbuster video game franchise.

item.title: Spotted a tiny black dot on your phone screen? It could be 'pixel cancer'
item.description: It starts with a tiny black dot on your phone screen. Then, before you know it, your device is down with a bad case of 'pixel cancer'. Read more.

item.title: This game was already 'incredible'. It just got another reason to play
item.description: It didn't need an expansion, but the fact it's getting one is all the more reason to dive back in.

item.title: How home audio and headphones are assisting hearing impaired
item.description: For years, hearing aids and high-end audio gear existed in separate worlds. But with the rise of AI that's all starting to change. Sonos' latest soundbar is bringing assistive hearing to the couch.

item.title: Google will not be forced to sell off Chrome or Android, US judge rules in landmark antitrust ruling
item.description: The decision overnight serves as a significant win for the tech company, which has avoided the most extreme possible outcome from a landmark antitrust case that determined the company was operating an illegal online search monopoly.

item.title: Apple say their new AirPods can translate in real time, does it actually work?
item.description: Last week Apple revealed their new smartphones, the iPhone 17 lineup, but another reveal was their new headphones, the AirPods Pro 3.

item.title: Recall notice issued for some Tesla battery systems
item.description: The ACCC issued a product recall of a Tesla battery after reports they can overheat and catch fire.

item.title: 'It's not the Pokemon you grew up playing': Pokemon Legends Z-A Preview
item.description: A preview has never changed my opinion as quickly as Pokemon Legends Z-A. I went in apathetic and walked out hungry for more. This isn't the Pokemon I grew up playing.

item.title: New game Ghost of Yōtei a ‘feast for the senses’
item.description: In a year overflowing with excellent samurai and ninja titles, Ghost of Yōtei had a big job to do.

item.title: LEGO Party offers fresh take on the classic Mario Party formula
item.description: New Aussie made video game LEGO Party takes the classic Mario Party formula and reimagines it with a LEGO twist.

item.title: Sonic Team's Takashi Iizuka on Sonic Racing: CrossWorlds
item.description: Sonic Team's Takashi Iizuka opens up about his move to Los Angeles to rebuild the Sonic name. Now, he's just released Sonic Racing: CrossWorlds, a high speed racing video game inviting characters from Microsoft, Nickelodeon and more.

item.title: Super Mario Galaxy and Super Mario Galaxy 2 bundle: Is it worth it?
item.description: It's $89.95 on the Australian eShop. Outrageously, they're available separately for $59.95! That's a lot of money for any 15-year-old game, even ones as good as Super Mario Galaxy.

item.title: Baby Steps creators take us step by step through the game's development
item.description: The video game world is currently buzzing about Baby Steps, the new release that's earning praise for being frustrating, awkward, and brilliant all at once. 9news.com.au met the team behind it.

item.title: More than 80,000 gamers to descend on Melbourne for PAX this week
item.description: Tens of thousands of gamers across the country are packing their bags, ready to spawn in Melbourne for PAX Australia 2025 this week. Here's what to expect.

item.title: Google launching 'most powerful AI search experience yet' in Australia
item.description: The search giant this morning announced it would be rolling out AI Mode, its "most powerful AI search experience yet", in Australia and more than 40 other countries this week.

item.title: 'A dangerous game to play': Are we really in an AI tech bubble and what happens if it bursts?
item.description: Even Sam Altman, the head of ChatGPT maker OpenAI, asked directly if investors were "overexcited about AI", flat out said "yes".

item.title: Xbox game takes arachnophobia mode to the next level in new update
item.description: The story of four teens shrunk down to bug size continues with Grounded 2, but this time the kids are trapped inside a huge park with new dangers and objectives. 9news.com.au caught up with the game's creator to learn all about it.

item.title: More than 1600 customers of Dodo and iPrimus impacted by cybersecurity breach
item.description: Another Aussie company has been hit by a cybersecurity breach, with more than 1600 customers of telco Dodo and iPrimus being impacted by a data breach.

item.title: Apple updates its premium iPad, MacBook Pro and even Vision Pro gets an upgrade
item.description: This week three new products hit the stores - all of them year on year upgrades featuring Apple's newest processor, the M5 Apple Silicon.

item.title: Halo coming to PlayStation for the first time in new remake: 'Built from the ground up'
item.description: Xbox and Halo Studios have officially announced a full remake of Halo: Combat Evolved, rebuilt from the ground up and launching on next year. 9news.com.au caught up with the creators.

item.title: Xania Monet is the first AI-powered artist to debut on a Billboard airplay chart, but she likely won't be the last
item.description: So far, Monet has appeared on multiple Billboard charts since first releasing a song in summer 2025, including the Hot Gospel Songs (for her song Let Go, Let God) and the Hot R&B Songs chart (for her song How Was I Supposed to Know), according to the publication.

item.title: Australian cyber experts warn of the 'catastrophic' threat quantum computing could pose
item.description: They have good reason to be nervous - the emergence of quantum computing looms as the biggest global security challenge in decades.

item.title: REVIEW: As a long-lapsed Pokemon fan, it feels like Legends Z-A was made for me. But this is my only wish for the new game
item.description: Now that the game is on store shelves, I'm happy to report that battles are still the best part of Pokemon Legends Z-A.

item.title: 'Double-edged sword': How Aussie content creators are reacting to the under-16 social media ban
item.description: As the under-16 social media ban approaches, Australian content creators have been left wondering how the restrictions will impact their careers, with some calling it a "double-edged sword."

item.title: ‘The zombies have evolved’: Interview with Resident Evil Requiem producer
item.description: Capcom, the developers of the classic Resident Evil franchise, are gearing up for their ninth major entry in the long running series — Resident Evil Requiem. 9news.com.au caught up with the game's producer.

item.title: Everything we know about ChatGPT's 'erotica' rollout in December
item.description: ChatGPT is about to get a lot hornier. Sam Altman, chief executive of the artificial intelligence chatbot's parent company, says it's about allowing adults more freedom to use AI how they want.

item.title: Review: The best game on the Switch 2 yet
item.description: It meets (and arguably exceeds) the exceptionally high bar set by its predecessors and is willing to gamble the franchise's small but loyal fanbase on a few big changes.

item.title: How Avatar: Frontiers of Pandora's From the Ashes expansion aligns with the new film
item.description: Developed in collaboration between Massive Entertainment and Lightstorm Entertainment, the From the Ashes game expansion runs side by side to the events of Avatar: Fire and Ash.

item.title: Apple issues urgent iPhone update after triple zero emergency call testing raises concerns
item.description: Apple has released an emergency update to iOS 26 that applies only to the iPhone 12, addressing a compatibility issue with Australia's emergency calling system.

item.title: The TVs of tomorrow: Ultra-thin designs and 130-inch monsters
item.description: CES, the biggest technology show in the world, is happening this week in Las Vegas, a place for the biggest brands and the newest startups to demonstrate their innovation to the world in the hope of making it into retail stores this year.

item.title: Australian technology companies making it big on the world stage
item.description: A﻿longside the tech giants at the CES trade show in Las Vegas are Australian start-ups displaying their products.

item.title: The most anticipated video games of 2026
item.description: 2026 is going to be see some incredible video games released - from the highly anticipated Grand Theft Auto VI to Xbox Game Studios' Fable.

item.title: Seven years on, Fallout 76 developers still listening to fans and now watching the TV series
item.description: Seven years after its launch, video game Fallout 76 continues to evolve, with new updates shaped by both fan feedback and inspiration from Amazon's Fallout TV series, as they reveal to 9news.com.au.

item.title: Create your own Pokemon paradise with the latest in Nintendo life-sim
item.description: After an hour rebuilding my own deserted island back from the brink, it's safe to say our favourite pocket monsters remain irresistibly charming.

item.title: New God of War game headlines 'epic' PlayStation State of Play event
item.description: A new God of War game, exciting trailers, and several major reveals — PlayStation's February State of Play was one of the strongest showcases in recent memory.

item.title: Samsung's new phone feature blocks prying eyes
item.description: Samsung's flagship smartphone for 2026 will launch with an industry-first privacy feature aimed at stopping those prying eyes around you from glimpsing what you're looking at on your screen.

item.title: Apple unveils $899 MacBook Neo in surprise launch
item.description: It's a brand new model for the tech giant, and it's a big shift away from the typical price point.

item.title: Apple's latest iPhone is designed for those clinging onto older phones
item.description: There are a lot of people using older model phones that are contemplating an upgrade. Could the newest version be the right fit?

item.title: Bigfoot, trombone, orca among the latest emojis
item.description: Have you ever wanted to send a text about orcas or Bigfoot or trombones only to find you have to do it using words instead of pictures?

item.title: Apple MacBook Neo review: The laptop market is redefined
item.description: When the low-cost MacBook Neo was announced last week, I had to look twice to check the price was in Australian dollars and not US dollars because a sub-$1000 laptop from Apple was unimaginable, until now.

item.title: Aussies heading to the USA set for high-speed Wi-Fi in a travel revolution
item.description: Australians heading to the United States are set for a big change in the way we travel, with United Airlines launching an all-new in-flight experience on their next-generation aircraft, including personalised infotainment and high-speed satellite internet.

item.title: Sydney Opera House sails light up to mark 50 Years of Apple
item.description: The sails of the Sydney Opera House will light up with colour over the next three nights as part of a global celebration of Apple's 50th birthday.

item.title: Apple almost went broke – now it's celebrating 50 years
item.description: H﻿ow the company came back from the brink to become a global juggernaut.

item.title: Big hint Apple's first foldable smartphone is coming this year
item.description: After years of rumours, it seems more likely than ever that Apple will introduce a foldable smartphone this year as part of a shake-up of its entire iPhone line-up this September.

item.title: Tech giant Microsoft unveiles $25 billion plan to reshape the Australian workplace
item.description: A﻿nd the man in charge says Australia is in a prime position for the major growth technology.

item.title: Today in History - May 31: Conman exposed by what he ordered from the menu
item.description: A long-time fraudster posing as a Saudi prince was jailed for 18 years on May 31, 2019.

item.title: Shirtless Aussie questioned over alleged cafe rampage in Vietnam
item.description: An Australian tourist is being questioned by Vietnamese police after allegedly going on a rampage in a busy cafe.

item.title: Hundreds arrested in violent clashes as fans celebrate Paris Saint-Germain's Champions League win
item.description: Police have detained 780 people involved in violent clashes in Paris and other French cities that erupted after Paris Saint-Germain won the Champions League title.

item.title: The 9News app is changing
item.description: The 9News app is changing
